svg.text: Markscheme answer
Q8
Part (a)
METHOD 1

text.font-family: Cambria Math, STIX Two Math, Times New Roman, serif

svg.text: Start with: Show that sin 2 nx = sin((2n + 1)x) cos x – cos((2n + 1)x) sin x.

text.font-family: Cambria Math, STIX Two Math, Times New Roman, serif

svg.text: Rearrange to standard form: ax^2 + bx + c = 0 (or equivalent).
Solve using factorisation / substitution / inverse operations.

text.font-family: Cambria Math, STIX Two Math, Times New Roman, serif

svg.text: For quadratics: x = (-b ± sqrt(b^2 - 4ac)) / (2a).
Check domain/restrictions and reject invalid roots.
Part (b)
METHOD 1

text.font-family: Cambria Math, STIX Two Math, Times New Roman, serif

svg.text: Start with: Hence prove, by induction, that cos x + cos 3x + cos 5x + ... + cos((2n – 1)x) = x
nx sin2 2sin , for all n  +, sin x ≠

text.font-family: Cambria Math, STIX Two Math, Times New Roman, serif

svg.text: Rearrange to standard form: ax^2 + bx + c = 0 (or equivalent).
Solve using factorisation / substitution / inverse operations.

text.font-family: Cambria Math, STIX Two Math, Times New Roman, serif

svg.text: For quadratics: x = (-b ± sqrt(b^2 - 4ac)) / (2a).
Check domain/restrictions and reject invalid roots.
Final answer: valid solution set.
METHOD 2
Alternative route: solve simultaneously after substitution or use graph/intersection.
Confirm both methods give the same valid value(s).
Part (c)
METHOD 1

text.font-family: Cambria Math, STIX Two Math, Times New Roman, serif

svg.text: Start with: Solve the equation cos x + cos 3x = 2 1 , 0 < x < π.

text.font-family: Cambria Math, STIX Two Math, Times New Roman, serif

svg.text: Rearrange to standard form: ax^2 + bx + c = 0 (or equivalent).
Solve using factorisation / substitution / inverse operations.

text.font-family: Cambria Math, STIX Two Math, Times New Roman, serif

svg.text: For quadratics: x = (-b ± sqrt(b^2 - 4ac)) / (2a).
Check domain/restrictions and reject invalid roots.
Final answer: valid solution set.
METHOD 2
Alternative route: solve simultaneously after substitution or use graph/intersection.
Confirm both methods give the same valid value(s).
